Youth participants 2025
FabLab

A creative haven at the heart of the community

The FabLab at the Salt Spring Public Library is a community makerspace equipped with 3D printers, laser cutters, design software, and more. It offers islanders of all ages a place to learn, experiment, and create -- all free of charge.

In 2025, the FabLab logged over 1,100 appointments and 3,600 drop-in visits, with STEAM program attendance over 1,000 participants.

“The Fab Lab is a very special place for my neurodivergent child and for our community in general.”
$700k+ over 5 years 97% booking rate
GISS Robotics

Building robots, building futures

The Cyber/Scorpions team brings together students from grades 8 to 12 who design, build, and compete through VEX Robotics. The team has grown to 30 students and seven independently designed robots in a single season.

FabLab

Introduction to 3D Printing

Learn to model, slice, and print your first object. No experience needed.

Apr 12, 2026 10am - 1pm 6 spots
Learn more
Youth STEAM

Drone Piloting for Teens

Fly, photograph, and map. Ages 12-18. Drones provided.

May 3, 2026 1pm - 4pm 10 spots
Learn more
Shoemaking

Leather Shoemaking: Monk Strap

A five-day intensive workshop covering pattern cutting, lasting, and hand-welting. Leave with your own pair of handmade shoes.

Apr 26 - 27 9am - 5pm 3 spots
Apply
Catalyst Grants

Helping artists finish what they started

Grants of $3,000 to $10,000 for visual artists across the Southern Gulf Islands. $100,000 committed annually in partnership with the Salt Spring National Art Prize.

“Smart arts funding” — a model that directly supports the working artist at the moment of greatest need.
Gulf Islands Driftwood editorial
